task_completion.description: Verify that the YDBGui web interface is open and accessible in a web browser.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window is open displaying the YDBGui interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent navigated to the RAD/NUC MED PROCEDURES file in YDBGui.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The YDBGui interface shows the global ^RA(71) selected or entered in the global search/navigation area.

task_completion.description: Verify that radiology procedure definitions are visible within the procedures global.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Data nodes, records, or text under the ^RA(71) global are expanded or displayed, showing procedure names or definition details.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent navigated to the RAD/NUC MED ORDERS file in YDBGui.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The YDBGui interface shows the global ^RA(75.1) selected or entered in the global search/navigation area.

task_completion.description: Verify that existing patient orders or details are visible within the orders global.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Data nodes, records, or text under the ^RA(75.1) global are expanded or displayed, showing order details.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the YDBGui web interface rather than terminal commands to view the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: The database exploration occurs within a web browser showing YDBGui, not in a terminal window running MUMPS commands directly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual YDBGui application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser UI elements, URL bar, and YDBGui interactive elements (like tree views or data tables) are visible and properly rendered, rather than a static text file or mock interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply type the expected global names into a text editor to simulate completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The globals ^RA(71) and ^RA(75.1) are seen inside the YDBGui data explorer, not in a standalone text editor, notes app, or terminal echo.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must use the YDBGui web interface to browse the VistA database. The RAD/NUC MED PROCEDURES file is located at the global ^RA(71). The RAD/NUC MED ORDERS file is located at the global ^RA(75.1).
